Section 1 — Executive Summary

1. Executive Summary

MOOVIZ is a real-time community delivery platform that connects senders who need packages delivered with available drivers in their area. The application operates in the Israeli market with full Hebrew RTL support, serving as a peer-to-peer logistics marketplace.

Project Overview

AttributeValue
Application NameMOOVIZ — Real-Time Community Delivery Platform (MVP)
ClientG.M. Mooviz Software (2026) Ltd
DeveloperKAL Solutions Group
CTOTamir Konortov — End-to-end technical ownership
Budget90,000 NIS + VAT (fixed price)
Timeline45 calendar days, 8 sprints
Current Versionv1.1.9
PlatformsiOS + Android (React Native)

Core Capabilities

  • Sender Flow: Create delivery requests with pickup/destination, item details, photos, pricing, and scheduling. Track deliveries in real-time. Approve/decline interested drivers. Confirm payment completion.
  • Driver Flow: Browse nearby deliveries via geohash-based proximity feed. Express interest, receive selection notifications. Proof photo system for pickup and delivery. Confirm payment receipt.
  • Multi-Driver Selection: Multiple drivers can express interest in a delivery. Sender selects preferred driver. Selected driver has 15 minutes to confirm acceptance.
  • Real-Time Chat: In-app messaging between sender and driver per delivery, with image sharing, system messages, and automatic 12-hour close after delivery completion.
  • Admin Dashboard: User management, delivery oversight, KYC approval/rejection, reports moderation, analytics dashboards, bulk email, and migration tools.
  • Push Notifications: 8 notification events covering the full delivery lifecycle, with custom sounds, quiet hours, and action buttons.

Milestone Completion

MilestoneScopePaymentStatus
M0 Setup & Scaffold 50% advance = 45,000 NIS COMPLETE
M1 Auth + Users + Data Models + Base Mobile 15% = 13,500 NIS COMPLETE
M2 Delivery Lifecycle + GPS + Chat + Push + Admin 10% = 9,000 NIS COMPLETE
M3 Ratings + Cancellation + Polish + QA 5% = 4,500 NIS COMPLETE
M4 Production Build + Store Submission 20% = 18,000 NIS IN PROGRESS
Section 2 — Technology Stack

2. Technology Stack

LayerTechnologyVersionNotes
Mobile Framework React Native (Bare Workflow) 0.75.5 No Expo managed workflow; bare RN with native modules
Navigation React Navigation 7.x Stack + Bottom Tabs navigators
State Management React Context + Firestore Listeners — No Redux; real-time via onSnapshot
Maps react-native-maps + Google Maps SDK 1.18.2 Google Places Autocomplete + Geohash proximity queries
Animations react-native-reanimated 3.15.5 Gesture-driven animations, Lottie integration
Gesture Handling react-native-gesture-handler 2.20.2 Swipeable cards, pull-to-refresh
Backend Firebase Cloud Functions (Gen 2) Node 18 TypeScript, onCall + triggers + scheduled
Database Cloud Firestore (NoSQL) — Real-time document database with offline persistence
Authentication Firebase Auth — Email+Password + Phone OTP (SMS verification)
Storage Firebase Cloud Storage — Images, videos, KYC documents; 5MB max per file
Push Notifications Firebase Cloud Messaging FCM v1 HTTP API Triggered by Cloud Functions on Firestore writes
Admin Dashboard React + Vite + Tailwind CSS — Single-page application (SPA)
Language TypeScript 5.x Strict mode everywhere (mobile, functions, admin, shared)
Monorepo pnpm Workspaces — public-hoist-pattern[]=* in .npmrc
iOS Build CocoaPods + Xcode — Firebase iOS SDK 11.11, modular headers
Android Build Gradle (AGP 8.5) compileSdk 34 Hermes engine, 16KB page-aligned
i18n Custom I18nContext — Hebrew-first RTL layout; English secondary
Theming Custom ThemeContext — Dark/light mode with design tokens

Key Pinned Dependencies

Version Pinning Notice The following dependencies are pinned to specific versions due to compatibility requirements with React Native 0.75.5. Upgrading any of these requires thorough testing on both platforms.
PackagePinned VersionReason
react-native-reanimated3.15.5RN 0.75.x compatibility
react-native-gesture-handler2.20.2Stable with reanimated 3.15.5
react-native-screens3.35.0Navigation 7.x compatibility
react-native-safe-area-context4.12.0Layout stability
react-native-maps1.18.2Google Maps SDK compatibility
Section 3 — Architecture Overview

3. Architecture Overview

MOOVIZ follows a three-tier serverless architecture built entirely on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) via Firebase. All business logic resides in Cloud Functions, ensuring the mobile client never directly mutates critical state.

Architecture Layers

Tier 1: Mobile Client (React Native)

  • Communicates with Firebase via the official React Native Firebase SDK (@react-native-firebase/*)
  • Real-time data via Firestore onSnapshot listeners — no polling
  • Offline persistence enabled via Firestore's built-in cache
  • All state-changing operations go through Cloud Functions (callable) — never direct writes for critical fields
  • Google Maps SDK for map rendering, Places API for address autocomplete
  • Geohash-based proximity queries for the driver feed (1–50km radius)
  • FCM for push notification reception; custom sound files per event type

Tier 2: Cloud Functions (Serverless Backend)

  • Callable Functions (22): HTTPS-callable endpoints for all user actions (create delivery, express interest, confirm pickup, etc.). Each validates authentication, role-based access control (RBAC), and state machine transitions server-side.
  • Firestore Triggers (3): React to document changes — send push notifications on status transitions, handle user creation defaults, and KYC status change events.
  • Scheduled Functions (4): Cron jobs for timeout cleanup (72h stale deliveries), selection timeout (15min), chat auto-close (12h inactive), and notification radius expansion (5min intervals).
  • All functions use Firestore transactions for atomic state mutations, preventing race conditions.

Tier 3: Admin Dashboard (React SPA)

  • React + Vite + Tailwind CSS single-page application
  • Direct Firestore access via Firebase JS SDK with admin-role security rules
  • User management, KYC approval, delivery oversight, report moderation
  • Analytics dashboards with period filters, regional breakdowns, and CSV exports
  • Bulk email broadcasting via callable Cloud Function

Data Flow

Mobile App (React Native)
    |
    |-- Firebase Auth (Email+Password / Phone OTP)
    |-- Firestore SDK (onSnapshot listeners for real-time data)
    |-- Cloud Functions SDK (callable for state mutations)
    |-- Cloud Storage SDK (image/video upload)
    |-- FCM SDK (push notification reception)
    |
Cloud Functions (Node 18, Gen 2)
    |
    |-- Firestore Admin SDK (read/write with elevated privileges)
    |-- FCM Admin SDK (send notifications)
    |-- Cloud Storage Admin (generate signed URLs)
    |-- Nodemailer (SMTP for bulk emails)
    |
Cloud Firestore (NoSQL Document Database)
    |
    |-- Security Rules (RBAC enforcement)
    |-- Real-time sync to all connected clients
    |-- Offline persistence (mobile)
    |
Admin Dashboard (React SPA)
    |
    |-- Firebase JS SDK (Firestore + Auth + Storage)
    |-- Admin-role security rules (elevated read/write)

Key Architectural Decisions

DecisionRationale
Server-side status validation All delivery status transitions validated in Cloud Functions. Client cannot directly write to status fields. Prevents tampering and race conditions.
Geohash proximity queries Firestore does not support native geo-queries. Geohash encoding (precision 6, ~1.2km grid) enables efficient range queries using string prefix matching.
Firestore real-time listeners No polling. onSnapshot provides sub-second updates for delivery status, chat messages, and driver locations.
Firestore transactions All status mutations use transactions to prevent race conditions (e.g., two drivers confirming simultaneously).
External payment processing Payments handled via deep links to Bit/PayBox apps. No in-app payment processing, avoiding PCI compliance overhead for MVP.
Dual-role users Users can be both sender and driver. activeMode field toggles between roles without separate accounts.
Session token enforcement Single-device login via sessionToken field. New login invalidates previous sessions.
Section 4 — Directory Structure

4. Directory Structure

Mooviz/
├── apps/
│   ├── mobile/                          # React Native app (iOS + Android)
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── screens/                 # Screen components organized by role
│   │   │   │   ├── auth/                # 8 auth screens (Login, Register, OTP, etc.)
│   │   │   │   ├── sender/              # 4 sender screens (Home, Create, MyDeliveries, Detail)
│   │   │   │   ├── driver/              # 4 driver screens (Feed, MyJobs, Detail, KYC)
│   │   │   │   ├── onboarding/          # 1 onboarding screen
│   │   │   │   └── shared/              # 5 shared screens (Chat, Profile, Rating, Map, CompleteProfile)
│   │   │   ├── components/              # 46 reusable UI components
│   │   │   ├── hooks/                   # 14 custom React hooks
│   │   │   ├── services/                # 11 Firebase service wrappers
│   │   │   ├── navigation/              # React Navigation config (RootNavigator, types)
│   │   │   ├── i18n/                    # Internationalization (Hebrew + English strings)
│   │   │   ├── theme/                   # Dark/light theme system (ThemeContext, tokens)
│   │   │   ├── utils/                   # 6 utility modules (formatters, validators, geohash, etc.)
│   │   │   ├── types/                   # TypeScript type definitions
│   │   │   └── constants/               # 6 constant files (config, colors, cities, statusConfig, etc.)
│   │   ├── android/                     # Android native project (Gradle, build.gradle)
│   │   │   └── app/
│   │   │       ├── build.gradle         # compileSdk 34, AGP 8.5, signing configs
│   │   │       └── src/main/
│   │   │           ├── AndroidManifest.xml
│   │   │           └── res/raw/         # Custom notification sounds (.mp3)
│   │   └── ios/                         # iOS native project (Xcode)
│   │       ├── MoovizMobile.xcodeproj
│   │       ├── MoovizMobile/
│   │       │   ├── Info.plist
│   │       │   ├── AppDelegate.mm
│   │       │   └── *.mp3                # Custom notification sounds
│   │       ├── Podfile                  # CocoaPods config (Firebase iOS SDK 11.11)
│   │       └── Pods/                    # CocoaPods dependencies
│   │
│   └── admin/                           # Admin dashboard (React + Vite)
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── pages/                   # 11 admin pages
│       │   ├── components/              # Admin UI components (CsvExport, etc.)
│       │   ├── hooks/                   # Admin hooks (useAnalytics, etc.)
│       │   ├── constants/               # Regions, configs
│       │   └── services/                # Firebase service layer
│       ├── index.html
│       └── vite.config.ts
│
├── functions/                           # Firebase Cloud Functions (TypeScript)
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── callable/                    # 5 callable modules (22 exported functions)
│   │   │   ├── deliveryCallable.ts      # Delivery lifecycle (create, interest, confirm, cancel)
│   │   │   ├── userCallable.ts          # User management (createUser, updateProfile, FCM tokens)
│   │   │   ├── kycCallable.ts           # KYC review (admin)
│   │   │   ├── emailCallable.ts         # Bulk email broadcasting
│   │   │   └── encryptionCallable.ts    # Authorized photo access, document decryption
│   │   ├── triggers/                    # 3 Firestore trigger modules
│   │   │   ├── deliveryTrigger.ts       # onDeliveryUpdate: push notifications + system messages
│   │   │   ├── userTrigger.ts           # onUserCreate + onUserUpdate
│   │   │   └── chatTrigger.ts           # Chat-related triggers
│   │   ├── scheduled/                   # 4 scheduled (cron) functions
│   │   │   ├── timeoutCleanup.ts        # Revert stale pending deliveries (72h)
│   │   │   ├── selectionTimeout.ts      # Revert unconfirmed selections (15min)
│   │   │   ├── chatAutoClose.ts         # Close inactive chats (12h)
│   │   │   └── notifyExpansion.ts       # Expand notification radius (5min intervals)
│   │   ├── validators/                  # Status transition + RBAC validation
│   │   │   └── statusValidator.ts
│   │   └── services/                    # Backend services
│   │       ├── notificationService.ts   # FCM push notification sender
│   │       └── geohashService.ts        # Geohash encoding/querying
│   ├── tsconfig.json
│   └── package.json
│
├── shared/                              # Shared types, constants, validators
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── types/                       # TypeScript interfaces (User, Delivery, Chat, Rating, Report)
│   │   ├── constants/                   # Status transitions, notification events
│   │   ├── validators/                  # Shared validation logic
│   │   └── index.ts                     # Barrel export
│   ├── dist/                            # Compiled output
│   └── package.json                     # @mooviz/shared
│
├── firestore.rules                      # Firestore security rules
├── storage.rules                        # Cloud Storage security rules
├── firebase.json                        # Firebase project configuration
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml                  # pnpm monorepo config
├── .npmrc                               # public-hoist-pattern[]=*
└── docs/                                # Documentation, architecture docs, ADRs
Section 5 — Data Model

5. Data Model (Firestore Collections)

All data is stored in Cloud Firestore, a NoSQL document database. Each collection is detailed below with field names, types, and descriptions. Timestamps use Firestore's native Timestamp type.

5.1   users/{userId}

Stores user profile, role, location, KYC status, and FCM tokens. Document ID matches Firebase Auth UID.

FieldTypeDescription
uidstringFirebase Auth UID (matches document ID)
fullNamestringUser's full legal name
nicknamestring?Optional display nickname
phonestringPhone number in E.164 format (e.g., +972501234567)
emailstring?Email address (from Firebase Auth)
citystringUser's city name (Hebrew)
role"sender" | "driver"Base user role (all users start as "sender")
activeMode"client" | "driver"Current active mode toggle (client = sender behavior)
driverAvailablebooleanWhether driver is currently available for deliveries
driverUnlockedbooleanWhether driver mode is unlocked (via KYC approval or migration)
profilePhotoURLstringCloud Storage URL for profile photo
kycDocumentURLstringCloud Storage URL for KYC driver's license document
kycIdURLstring?Cloud Storage URL for KYC ID document
kycStatus"pending" | "approved" | "rejected"KYC verification status (admin-controlled)
ratingAsDriver{ average: number, count: number }Aggregate rating when acting as driver
ratingAsSender{ average: number, count: number }Aggregate rating when acting as sender
completedDeliveriesnumberTotal completed deliveries count
status"active" | "suspended" | "blocked"Account status (admin-controlled)
fcmTokensstring[]Array of FCM device tokens for push notifications
location{ lat, lng, geohash }Last known location with geohash for proximity queries
sessionTokenstring?Current session token for single-device enforcement
genderstring?User's gender (optional profile field)
ageRangestring?User's age range (optional profile field)
acceptedTermsAtTimestamp?When the user accepted terms of service
migratedFromstring?Source ID if migrated from Glide platform
createdAtTimestampAccount creation timestamp
updatedAtTimestamp?Last profile update timestamp

5.2   deliveries/{deliveryId}

Core delivery document containing full lifecycle data, participants, proof photos, and the interested drivers list.

FieldTypeDescription
senderIdstringUID of the sender who created the delivery
senderNamestring?Denormalized sender display name
senderPhotoUrlstring?Denormalized sender profile photo URL
senderRatingnumber?Denormalized sender rating at time of creation
driverIdstring?UID of the assigned driver (null until assigned)
driverNamestring?Denormalized driver display name
driverPhotoUrlstring?Denormalized driver profile photo URL
driverRatingnumber?Denormalized driver rating
chatIdstring?Reference to the associated chat document
statusDeliveryStatusCurrent status (see State Machine, Section 6)
pickupGeoPointPickup location: { address, city, lat, lng, geohash }
destinationGeoPointDestination location: { address, city, lat, lng, geohash }
itemDeliveryItemItem details: { description, size }
mediaURLsstring[]Array of item photo/video Cloud Storage URLs
pricenumberDelivery price in NIS
pickupDateTimestamp | "asap"Scheduled pickup date or ASAP flag
timeRangestring?Preferred time range (e.g., "morning", "afternoon")
notesstring?Free-text sender notes for the driver
payment{ senderConfirmed, driverConfirmed }Payment confirmation flags (both must be true)
proof{ pickupURL?, deliveryURL?, paymentURL? }Proof photo URLs for pickup, delivery, and payment
interestedDriversInterestedDriver[]Array of drivers who expressed interest (multi-driver system)
selectedDriverIdstring?UID of sender's selected driver (pending confirmation)
selectionExpiresAtTimestamp?When the 15-minute selection confirmation window expires
statusHistoryStatusEntry[]Array of all status transitions: { status, timestamp, actor, note? }
cancelledBystring?UID of the user who cancelled (if applicable)
ratedboolean?Whether rating has been submitted
ratedBySenderboolean?Whether sender submitted rating
ratedByDriverboolean?Whether driver submitted rating
ratingsVisibleAtTimestamp?When mutual ratings become visible (after both rate)
notifiedDriversstring[]UIDs of drivers who were notified about this delivery
notifyRadiusnumberCurrent notification radius in km (expands over time)
notifyExpansionCountnumberNumber of radius expansions performed
lastNotifyExpansionTimestamp?When the last radius expansion was performed
timeoutAtTimestampWhen this delivery will be auto-cleaned (72h default)
createdAtTimestampDelivery creation timestamp
updatedAtTimestampLast update timestamp
InterestedDriver Sub-Object Each entry in interestedDrivers[] contains: uid, name, photoUrl, rating, completedDeliveries, distanceKm, expressedAt (Timestamp), and status ("interested" | "selected" | "confirmed" | "declined" | "cancelled" | "withdrawn").

5.3   chats/{chatId}

Chat thread metadata. One chat per delivery. Messages stored in a subcollection.

FieldTypeDescription
participantsstring[]Array of two UIDs [senderId, driverId]
deliveryIdstringReference to the associated delivery
lastMessagestringPreview text of the last message
lastMessageAtTimestampTimestamp of the last message
lastSenderIdstringUID of the last message sender
lastReadBy{ [uid]: Timestamp }Map of user UIDs to their last-read timestamps
closedbooleanWhether chat is closed (auto-closed after 12h inactivity)
closedAtTimestamp?When the chat was closed

chats/{chatId}/messages/{messageId} (Subcollection)

FieldTypeDescription
senderIdstringUID of the message sender (or "system" for automated messages)
senderNamestringDisplay name of the sender
textstringMessage content
imageUrlstring?Cloud Storage URL if message contains an image
type"text" | "image" | "system"Message type (system = auto-generated status updates)
createdAtTimestampMessage timestamp

5.4   ratings/{ratingId}

Individual rating records. Both sender and driver can rate each other after delivery.

FieldTypeDescription
fromUserIdstringUID of the rater
toUserIdstringUID of the rated user
deliveryIdstringAssociated delivery ID
ratingnumber (1-5)Star rating value
commentstring?Optional text comment
role"sender" | "driver"Role of the rater
createdAtTimestampRating submission timestamp

5.5   reports/{reportId}

User reports for moderation. Created by users, resolved by admins.

FieldTypeDescription
reporterIdstringUID of the reporter
reporterNamestringDisplay name of the reporter
reporterPhonestringReporter's phone number
reportedUserIdstringUID of the reported user
reportedUserNamestringDisplay name of the reported user
reportedRolestringRole of the reported user
deliveryIdstringAssociated delivery ID
categorystringReport category (e.g., "fraud", "abuse", "no_show")
descriptionstringDetailed report description
statusstringReport status ("open", "investigating", "resolved", "dismissed")
moderationActionstring?Action taken (e.g., "warning", "suspend", "block")
moderatorNotestring?Internal moderator notes
resolvedBystring?UID of the admin who resolved
createdAtTimestampReport creation timestamp
resolvedAtTimestamp?Resolution timestamp
Section 6 — Delivery Status State Machine

6. Delivery Status State Machine

Every delivery follows a strict state machine enforced server-side in Cloud Functions. The assertValidTransition() validator checks both the transition validity and the actor's RBAC role before allowing any status change.

Status Types (9 Total)

StatusDisplay NameColorDescription
NEW New #2196F3 Delivery just created, visible in driver feed. Waiting for driver interest.
PENDING Pending Approval #FF9800 One or more drivers expressed interest. Waiting for sender to select a driver.
AWAITING_CONFIRM Awaiting Driver Confirmation #FF6F00 Sender selected a driver. Driver has 15 minutes to confirm acceptance.
WAITING_FOR_PICKUP Waiting for Pickup #7B1FA2 Driver confirmed. En route to pickup location.
PICKED_UP Picked Up #00BCD4 Driver picked up the package (proof photo required). En route to destination.
DELIVERED Delivered #8BC34A Package delivered to destination (proof photo required).
AWAITING_PAYMENT Awaiting Payment #F57C00 Delivery complete, waiting for both parties to confirm payment.
COMPLETED_PAID Completed & Paid #4CAF50 Both parties confirmed payment. Terminal state.
CANCELLED Cancelled #F44336 Delivery cancelled (only allowed pre-pickup). Terminal state.

Transition Map

STATUS_TRANSITIONS = {
  new:                ["pending", "awaiting_confirm", "cancelled"],
  pending:            ["awaiting_confirm", "waiting_for_pickup", "new", "cancelled"],
  awaiting_confirm:   ["waiting_for_pickup", "pending", "new", "cancelled"],
  waiting_for_pickup: ["picked_up", "new", "cancelled"],
  picked_up:          ["delivered"],
  delivered:          ["awaiting_payment"],
  awaiting_payment:   ["completed_paid", "cancelled"],
  completed_paid:     [],   // terminal
  cancelled:          [],   // terminal
}

RBAC — Who Can Trigger Each Transition

TransitionAllowed ActorsTrigger
new → pendingDriverDriver expresses interest (expressInterest)
new → awaiting_confirmSenderSender selects driver directly
new → cancelledSender, SystemSender cancels or timeout cleanup
pending → awaiting_confirmSenderSender selects from interested drivers (selectDriver)
pending → newSender, DriverDriver withdraws interest or sender declines all
pending → waiting_for_pickupSenderSender approves driver (legacy single-driver flow)
pending → cancelledSender, Driver, SystemEither party cancels or timeout
awaiting_confirm → waiting_for_pickupDriverDriver confirms selection (confirmSelection)
awaiting_confirm → pendingDriver, SystemDriver declines or 15-min timeout
awaiting_confirm → newSender, Driver, SystemCancel selected driver
awaiting_confirm → cancelledSenderSender cancels delivery
waiting_for_pickup → picked_upDriverDriver confirms pickup + proof photo (confirmPickup)
waiting_for_pickup → newSenderSender removes driver pre-pickup
waiting_for_pickup → cancelledSender, DriverEither party cancels pre-pickup
picked_up → deliveredDriverDriver confirms delivery + proof photo (confirmDelivery)
delivered → awaiting_paymentSender, DriverFirst party confirms payment (confirmPayment)
awaiting_payment → completed_paidSender, Driver, SystemSecond party confirms payment
awaiting_payment → cancelledSystemSystem timeout (edge case)
Post-Pickup Irreversibility Once a delivery reaches picked_up status, it cannot be cancelled. The only valid forward path is: picked_up → delivered → awaiting_payment → completed_paid. This protects both parties after physical custody of the package has changed hands.

Multi-Driver Interest Flow

  1. Express Interest: Multiple drivers call expressInterest. Each is added to the interestedDrivers[] array with status "interested".
  2. Sender Selection: Sender reviews interested drivers (profile, rating, distance) and calls selectDriver. Delivery moves to awaiting_confirm. Selected driver entry status becomes "selected".
  3. 15-Minute Confirmation Window: selectionExpiresAt is set. The selectionTimeout scheduled function checks every 5 minutes and reverts to new if the window expires.
  4. Driver Confirms: Selected driver calls confirmSelection. Delivery moves to waiting_for_pickup. Driver entry status becomes "confirmed".
  5. Driver Declines: Selected driver calls declineSelection. Delivery reverts. Sender can select another driver from the remaining interested list.
Section 7 — Cloud Functions

7. Cloud Functions (26 Total)

All Cloud Functions are written in TypeScript, running on Node 18 (Gen 2). Callable functions require Firebase Auth and validate RBAC. All status mutations use Firestore transactions.

7.1   Callable Functions (22)

deliveryCallable.ts — Delivery Lifecycle (15 functions)

FunctionActorDescription
createDeliverySenderCreates a new delivery with pickup/destination, item details, price, scheduling. Initializes notification radius expansion fields.
expressInterestDriverDriver expresses interest in a delivery. Adds to interestedDrivers[] array. Transitions new → pending on first interest.
withdrawInterestDriverDriver withdraws their interest before selection. Removes from array. Reverts to new if no interested drivers remain.
withdrawFromInterestDriverAlias/variant of withdrawInterest for multi-driver flow.
selectDriverSenderSender selects a driver from the interested list. Sets selectedDriverId and selectionExpiresAt (15 min). Transitions to awaiting_confirm.
confirmSelectionDriverSelected driver confirms acceptance within 15-min window. Assigns driverId, creates chat, transitions to waiting_for_pickup.
declineSelectionDriverSelected driver declines. Reverts to pending (or new if no other interested drivers).
cancelSelectedDriverSenderSender cancels their driver selection before confirmation. Reverts delivery status.
approveDriverSenderLegacy single-driver approval. Directly assigns driver and transitions to waiting_for_pickup.
declineDriverSenderSender declines a specific interested driver. Removes from array.
confirmPickupDriverDriver confirms package pickup. Requires proof photo URL. Transitions waiting_for_pickup → picked_up.
confirmDeliveryDriverDriver confirms package delivery. Requires proof photo URL. Transitions picked_up → delivered.
confirmPaymentSender/DriverEither party confirms payment receipt. When both confirm, transitions to completed_paid. Increments completedDeliveries counter.
cancelDeliverySender/DriverCancels delivery (pre-pickup only). Uses transaction to prevent race with confirmSelection. Sets cancelledBy.
submitRatingSender/DriverSubmits a 1-5 star rating with optional comment. Updates aggregate ratingAsDriver/ratingAsSender on the rated user's profile. Prevents double submission.

userCallable.ts — User Management (4 functions)

FunctionActorDescription
createUserAuth'd UserCreates user profile document in Firestore after Firebase Auth signup. Sets defaults for all fields.
updateProfileAuth'd UserUpdates user profile fields (name, city, email, etc.). Validates allowed fields.
updateFCMTokenAuth'd UserAdds or updates FCM device token in fcmTokens[] array for push notifications.
removeFCMTokenAuth'd UserRemoves FCM token on logout or device change.

kycCallable.ts — KYC Admin (1 function)

FunctionActorDescription
reviewKYCAdminApproves or rejects driver KYC. Sets kycStatus, driverUnlocked. Sends push notification to the user.

emailCallable.ts — Email (1 function)

FunctionActorDescription
sendBulkEmailAdminSends HTML email to all users or a filtered subset. Uses SMTP (Hostinger) via Nodemailer.

encryptionCallable.ts — Secure Access (2 functions)

FunctionActorDescription
uploadProfilePhotoAuth'd UserHandles profile photo upload with server-side validation (size, type).
getAuthorizedPhotoAuth'd UserGenerates time-limited signed URL for accessing protected photos (KYC docs, proof photos).

7.2   Firestore Triggers (3 modules)

ModuleTriggerDescription
deliveryTrigger.ts onDocumentUpdated("deliveries/{id}") Fires on every delivery document update. Detects status transitions and sends appropriate push notifications (8 event types). Inserts system chat messages for status changes (e.g., "Driver has picked up your package").
userTrigger.ts onDocumentCreated("users/{id}")
onDocumentUpdated("users/{id}")		 onCreate: sets default fields, sends welcome notification. onUpdate: detects KYC status changes, notifies user of approval/rejection.
chatTrigger.ts Chat-related triggers Handles chat message events and metadata updates.

7.3   Scheduled Functions (4)

FunctionScheduleDescription
timeoutCleanup Every hour Queries deliveries with timeoutAt < now in eligible statuses (new, pending, awaiting_confirm, awaiting_payment). Reverts stale deliveries to cancelled or new. Default timeout: 24 hours (configurable per status).
selectionTimeout Every 5 minutes Checks awaiting_confirm deliveries where selectionExpiresAt < now. Reverts to new and marks selected driver as "cancelled". Sends notification to sender.
chatAutoClose Every hour Finds chats where lastMessageAt is more than 12 hours ago and the associated delivery is in a terminal state. Sets closed: true and closedAt.
notifyExpansion Every 5 minutes For new deliveries with no interested drivers, gradually expands the notification radius (e.g., 5km → 10km → 20km → 50km) and notifies additional drivers in the expanded area.

7.4   Backend Services

ServiceFileDescription
Notification Service services/notificationService.ts Centralized FCM push notification sender. Reads fcmTokens[] array from user document. Supports cached tokens/delivery data for performance. Handles token cleanup on send failure.
Geohash Service services/geohashService.ts Geohash encoding, neighbor calculation, and range query generation for proximity searches.
Section 8 — Push Notifications

8. Push Notifications (8 Events)

All push notifications are triggered by Cloud Functions (primarily the deliveryTrigger) via Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM v1 HTTP API). Notifications are sent to all devices in the user's fcmTokens[] array.

#EventRecipientTriggerCustom Sound
1 new_listing_nearby Drivers within geohash radius New delivery created + notifyExpansion scheduled function new_delivery.mp3
2 driver_interested Sender (delivery owner) Driver calls expressInterest driver_interested.mp3
3 driver_selected / sender_approved Driver (selected/assigned) Sender calls selectDriver or approveDriver success.mp3
4 delivery_picked_up Sender Delivery status → picked_up success.mp3
5 delivery_delivered Sender Delivery status → delivered success.mp3
6 payment_confirmed Both parties Delivery status → completed_paid payment.mp3
7 delivery_cancelled Other party Delivery status → cancelled error.mp3
8 new_chat_message Recipient (other party) New message in chat subcollection question.mp3

Sound Files

Custom notification sounds are bundled in both platforms:

  • Android: android/app/src/main/res/raw/ — MP3 files
  • iOS: ios/MoovizMobile/ — MP3 files added to Xcode bundle resources
Sound FileUsed For
new_delivery.mp3New delivery nearby notification
driver_interested.mp3Driver expressed interest
success.mp3Positive events (approved, picked up, delivered)
payment.mp3Payment confirmed
error.mp3Negative events (cancelled)
question.mp3Chat messages

Notification Configuration

  • FCM API: v1 HTTP API (not legacy)
  • Token Storage: Array of tokens per user (fcmTokens[]) supporting multiple devices
  • Token Cleanup: Failed tokens are automatically removed from the array
  • Android 13+: POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission requested at runtime via useNotifications hook
  • iOS: APNs certificate required for push delivery (development + production)
  • Exclusions: Notification sender is excluded from receiving their own notification
Section 9 — Security

9. Security

9.1   Authentication

  • Firebase Auth handles all authentication. Two methods supported:
    • Email + Password: Primary registration method. Email verification required.
    • Phone OTP (SMS): Secondary verification. 6-digit code, 60-second resend cooldown.
  • Two-Factor: Users must verify both email and phone number to complete profile.
  • Session Management: sessionToken field enforces single-device login. New login on another device invalidates previous session.
  • Language: auth.languageCode = 'he' set before sending verification/reset emails for Hebrew templates.

9.2   Firestore Security Rules

CollectionReadWriteNotes
users Own profile + admin Own non-protected fields + admin Protected fields (role, status, kycStatus) cannot be client-written
deliveries Sender (own) + Drivers (available) + Admin Via Cloud Functions only (for status fields) Sender can read own deliveries; drivers can read new/pending deliveries
chats Participants only + Admin Participants (messages subcollection) Chat document read restricted to participants[] array members
ratings Authenticated users Via Cloud Function (submitRating) Prevents rating manipulation
reports Reporter (own) + Admin Create: authenticated; Update: admin only Users can create reports but cannot modify after submission

9.3   Storage Security Rules

  • KYC Documents: Only the document owner can upload. Admin can read. Path: kyc/{userId}/*
  • Profile Photos: Owner upload only. Authenticated read. Path: profiles/{userId}/*
  • Delivery Media: Sender/driver upload. Authenticated read. Path: deliveries/{deliveryId}/*
  • File Size Limit: 5MB maximum per file
  • Content Type: Restricted to image/jpeg and image/png

9.4   Server-Side Validation

  • Status Transitions: assertValidTransition() validates every state change against the transition map and RBAC rules
  • Role Verification: assertUserRole() checks activeMode and driverUnlocked before allowing driver actions
  • Ownership Checks: assertIsSender() and assertIsDriver() verify the caller is the delivery participant
  • Active Account: assertUserActive() blocks suspended/blocked users from performing actions
  • KYC Verification: assertDriverApproved() ensures KYC approval before driver actions (with grandfather clause for Glide-migrated drivers)
  • Transaction Safety: All status mutations use Firestore transactions to prevent race conditions

9.5   Data Protection

  • Phone numbers stored in E.164 format with validation
  • No PII in Cloud Function logs
  • HTTPS enforced on all connections
  • KYC documents accessible only via time-limited signed URLs (getAuthorizedPhoto)
  • FCM tokens are per-device; removed on logout via removeFCMToken
  • google-services.json and .env files are gitignored
Section 10 — Mobile App Screens

10. Mobile App Screens (22 Total)

10.1   Auth Screens (8)

ScreenFilePurpose
LoginScreen screens/auth/LoginScreen.tsx Email + password login. Social login buttons (disabled for MVP). "Forgot Password" link. Navigation to Register.
RegisterScreen screens/auth/RegisterScreen.tsx Email + password registration. Full name, city selector. Triggers email verification on success.
OTPScreen screens/auth/OTPScreen.tsx 6-digit OTP code entry for phone verification. Auto-submit on complete. Resend timer (60s cooldown).
AddPhoneScreen screens/auth/AddPhoneScreen.tsx Phone number input with E.164 validation. Sends OTP via Firebase Auth phone provider.
EmailVerificationScreen screens/auth/EmailVerificationScreen.tsx Waiting screen for email verification. Polls for verification status. Resend link.
ForgotPasswordScreen screens/auth/ForgotPasswordScreen.tsx Password reset email request. Hebrew language template.
CompleteProfileScreen screens/auth/CompleteProfileScreen.tsx Post-registration profile completion: photo upload, city, gender, age range.
AcceptTermsScreen screens/auth/AcceptTermsScreen.tsx Terms of service acceptance gate. Must accept before accessing the app. Sets acceptedTermsAt.

10.2   Sender Screens (4)

ScreenFilePurpose
HomeScreen screens/sender/HomeScreen.tsx Sender dashboard. Shows active deliveries count, recent deliveries, unread chat banner. Quick-create delivery button.
CreateDeliveryScreen screens/sender/CreateDeliveryScreen.tsx Multi-step delivery creation: pickup/destination map picker with Places autocomplete, item description, size selector, media upload (photos/video), price input, date picker (ASAP or scheduled with time range chips), notes.
MyDeliveriesScreen screens/sender/MyDeliveriesScreen.tsx List of all sender's deliveries with status filter tabs. FlatList virtualization for performance. Pull-to-refresh. Navigation to detail.
DeliveryDetailScreen screens/sender/DeliveryDetailScreen.tsx Full delivery detail: live map tracking, status timeline, interested drivers list (with selection), proof photos gallery, payment confirmation (Bit/PayBox deep links), rating prompt, report button. Chat access.

10.3   Driver Screens (4)

ScreenFilePurpose
FeedScreen screens/driver/FeedScreen.tsx Nearby delivery feed with animated radar. Geohash proximity queries (1-50km slider). Size/distance preference filters. Available toggle. Card-based delivery list with express-interest button.
MyJobsScreen screens/driver/MyJobsScreen.tsx List of driver's assigned/active jobs with status filter tabs. Similar layout to MyDeliveries.
DeliveryDetailScreen screens/driver/DeliveryDetailScreen.tsx Driver's view of delivery detail: navigation to pickup/destination (maps deep link), proof camera for pickup/delivery, selection countdown banner (15min), payment confirmation, rating prompt, report button.
DriverKYCScreen screens/driver/DriverKYCScreen.tsx KYC document upload: driver's license and ID photo. Camera or gallery picker. Upload to Cloud Storage. Status tracking (pending/approved/rejected).

10.4   Onboarding (1)

ScreenFilePurpose
OnboardingScreen screens/onboarding/OnboardingScreen.tsx First-launch onboarding slides introducing MOOVIZ features. Swipeable pages with Lottie animations.

10.5   Shared Screens (5)

ScreenFilePurpose
ChatScreen screens/shared/ChatScreen.tsx Real-time chat between sender and driver. Text + image messages. System messages for status changes. Batch writes for performance. Location sharing throttled (30s). "Chat closed" banner when auto-closed.
ProfileScreen screens/shared/ProfileScreen.tsx User profile view and edit. Profile photo, name, city, contact info. KYC document viewer. Rating history. Mode switcher (sender/driver). Settings drawer.
CompleteProfileScreen screens/shared/CompleteProfileScreen.tsx Shared profile completion screen (used in flows beyond auth).
RatingScreen screens/shared/RatingScreen.tsx Star rating (1-5) with comment input. Prevents double submission via ratingSubmitted guard. Hides rate button after submission.
FullScreenMapScreen screens/shared/FullScreenMapScreen.tsx Full-screen map view for delivery pickup/destination locations. Used from detail screens.
Section 11 — Reusable Components

11. Reusable Components (46 Total)

ComponentDescription
AddressAutocompleteGoogle Places autocomplete input for pickup/destination address selection with map preview.
AnimatedButtonPressable button with scale/opacity animation feedback using react-native-reanimated.
AppAlertBranded alert dialog replacement for native Alert. Themed with MOOVIZ colors and RTL support.
AvailabilityToggleDriver availability switch. Persists driverAvailable to Firestore. Controls feed visibility.
AvatarCircleCircular profile photo with fallback initials. Supports various sizes.
BadgeGeneric badge component for counts, labels, and status indicators.
ButtonPrimary button component with loading state, disabled state, and theme variants.
CarAlertDelivery-specific alert with car icon animation.
CardBase card container with shadow, border radius, and theme-aware background.
ChatDeliveryBannerBanner showing unread chat count, links to active delivery chat.
DatePickerInputNative date picker with ASAP option and time range chips (morning, afternoon, evening).
DeliveryCardDelivery list item card. Shows pickup/destination, price, status badge, item size. Memoized with React.memo for FlatList performance.
DriverApprovalCardCard showing interested driver info with approve/decline actions for sender.
DriverConfirmBannerTop banner on driver detail screen showing 15-minute countdown for selection confirmation.
DriverOnboardingOnboarding flow for new drivers explaining KYC requirements.
DriverProfileModalModal displaying driver profile: photo, rating, completed deliveries, vehicle info.
EmptyStateEmpty list state with illustration and call-to-action. Used across feed, deliveries, jobs lists.
ErrorBoundaryReact error boundary wrapping every screen. Catches render errors with retry capability.
GlassCardGlassmorphism-style card with blur effect for premium UI elements.
ImageGalleryModalFull-screen image viewer for proof photos, item photos. Pinch-to-zoom, swipe navigation.
InterestedDriversListScrollable list of interested drivers with profile modals, select button. Used in sender delivery detail.
LiveMapViewReal-time map showing driver location, pickup/destination markers. FAB buttons for recenter and directions.
LoadingOverlayFull-screen semi-transparent loading overlay with spinner.
LoadingScreenFull-screen loading state with MOOVIZ branding.
LoadingTruckAnimated truck loading indicator (Lottie animation).
MapPickerInteractive map for selecting pickup/destination. Draggable marker with reverse geocoding.
ModalBase modal component with backdrop, close button, and animated entry.
ModeSwitcherToggle between sender and driver mode. Updates activeMode in Firestore.
OfflineBannerTop banner displayed when device is offline. Monitors network status via useNetworkStatus.
ProofCameraCamera capture component for proof photos (pickup/delivery). Validates and compresses before upload.
RatingStarsInteractive star rating input (1-5) and display-only mode.
RatingsHistoryModalModal showing user's rating history with comments.
ReportModalReport form modal: category selector, description input. Creates report document in Firestore.
ScreenHeaderConsistent screen header with back button, title, and optional right actions.
SelectionCountdownAnimated countdown timer for the 15-minute driver selection confirmation window.
SenderOnboardingOnboarding flow for new senders explaining how to create deliveries.
SenderProfileModalModal displaying sender profile: photo, rating, completed deliveries.
SettingsDrawerSide drawer with app settings: theme toggle, language, notifications, logout.
SkeletonLoaderAnimated placeholder skeletons for loading states across lists and detail screens.
StatusBadgeDelivery status badge with color coding matching the state machine colors.
StatusIndicatorSmall status dot indicator for user online/offline state.
TabHeaderTab bar header for filtered list views (e.g., delivery status tabs).
TabIconsBottom tab navigation icon set with badge counts.
TextInputBase text input with label, error display, RTL support, and validation.
ThemedInputTheme-aware input variant with dark/light mode support.
VerificationStepperStep indicator for the auth verification flow (email → phone → profile → terms).
Section 12 — Custom Hooks & Services

12. Custom Hooks (14 Total)

HookFileDescription
useAuth hooks/useAuth.ts Authentication state management. Provides current user, loading state, login/logout/register functions. Wraps Firebase Auth listener.
useChat hooks/useChat.ts Real-time chat for a specific delivery. Subscribes to messages subcollection via onSnapshot. Provides send message, send image, mark as read functions.
useChatList hooks/useChatList.ts Lists all user's active chats with unread counts. Cached user/delivery lookups to eliminate N+1 queries. Real-time via onSnapshot.
useDelivery hooks/useDelivery.ts Real-time subscription to a single delivery document. Provides all delivery data, status, participants, interested drivers list.
useDriverAvailability hooks/useDriverAvailability.ts Manages driver's available/unavailable toggle. Persists to Firestore. Does NOT reset on unmount (persistent preference).
useDriverEarnings hooks/useDriverEarnings.ts Fetches driver's completed delivery earnings. Uses get() with limit(200) instead of onSnapshot for performance.
useDriverLocationTracking hooks/useDriverLocationTracking.ts Two-tier GPS tracking: idle mode (5min intervals, 200m distance filter) and active delivery mode (1min intervals, 50m filter). Syncs to Firestore with geohash.
useFirestore hooks/useFirestore.ts Generic Firestore hook for collection/document subscriptions. Base hook used by others.
useInAppReview hooks/useInAppReview.ts Triggers native in-app review prompt after successful delivery completion.
useLocation hooks/useLocation.ts Current device location with permission handling. Used for initial map centering and distance calculations.
useNetworkStatus hooks/useNetworkStatus.ts Network connectivity monitoring. Drives OfflineBanner visibility. Handles offline-to-online transitions.
useNotifications hooks/useNotifications.ts FCM setup: requests permission (Android 13+ POST_NOTIFICATIONS), gets device token, registers/updates in Firestore. Handles foreground message display.
useSenderExpenses hooks/useSenderExpenses.ts Fetches sender's delivery expense history. Uses get() with limit(200) for performance.
useSound hooks/useSound.tsx Crash-proof sound playback with kill switch. Plays custom notification sounds. Gracefully degrades on failure.

13. Service Layer (11 Modules)

ServiceFileDescription
firebaseservices/firebase.tsFirebase app initialization. Exports auth, db, storage, functions instances.
authservices/auth.tsAuthentication wrappers: signIn, signUp, signOut, sendOTP, verifyOTP, resetPassword, updateProfile.
chatservices/chat.tsChat operations: send message, send image, mark read, create chat document.
storageservices/storage.tsCloud Storage uploads: profile photos, KYC docs, delivery media, proof photos. Compression via react-native-compressor.
notificationsservices/notifications.tsClient-side notification handling: foreground display, navigation on tap, sound playback.
navigationservices/navigation.tsNavigation service for deep linking and notification-triggered navigation.
soundservices/sound.tsSound player service with error recovery and kill switch for crash-proofing.
geohashservices/geohash.tsClient-side geohash encoding for location updates.
analyticsservices/analytics.tsAnalytics event tracking wrapper.
encryptionservices/encryption.tsClient-side encryption helpers for secure document access.
offlineQueueservices/offlineQueue.tsOffline action queue for operations performed while disconnected.
Section 14 — Admin Dashboard

14. Admin Dashboard (11 Pages)

The admin dashboard is a React + Vite + Tailwind CSS single-page application. It uses Firebase JS SDK for direct Firestore access with admin-role security rules. Authentication is email+password (admin accounts pre-seeded).

PageFileDescription
LoginPage pages/LoginPage.tsx Admin email+password login. Redirects to dashboard on success.
DashboardPage pages/DashboardPage.tsx Analytics overview: total users, deliveries, revenue. Period filter (7d/30d/90d/all). User breakdown (senders vs drivers). Regional distribution (Israel cities mapped to regions). Delivery timing charts. Monthly trend charts. Cashflow summary. CSV export on all data views.
UsersPage pages/UsersPage.tsx User list with search, role filter, status filter. Sortable columns. Click through to user detail.
UserDetailPage pages/UserDetailPage.tsx Full user profile view. KYC document viewer with approve/reject actions. Status management (active/suspend/block). Delivery history. Rating history. FCM token list.
DeliveriesPage pages/DeliveriesPage.tsx Delivery list with status filter tabs, date range filter, search. Shows all deliveries across all users.
DeliveryDetailPage pages/DeliveryDetailPage.tsx Full delivery detail: participants, status history timeline, proof photos, payment status, interested drivers list, chat messages.
ChatsPage pages/ChatsPage.tsx All active chats list. Click through to read messages. Identifies closed vs open chats.
ReportsPage pages/ReportsPage.tsx User reports moderation: open/investigating/resolved/dismissed tabs. Actions: investigate (set status), resolve with moderation action (warning, suspend, block), dismiss. Moderator notes.
EmailPage pages/EmailPage.tsx Bulk email broadcasting. Recipient selection (all users, by role, custom list). HTML email editor. Sends via sendBulkEmail callable function. SMTP: Hostinger (smtp.hostinger.com:465, SSL).
SettingsPage pages/SettingsPage.tsx Admin settings and configuration.
MigrationPage pages/MigrationPage.tsx Data migration tools for Glide-to-Firestore migration. User import, delivery import, image URL migration.

Admin Analytics Features

  • Period Filters: 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, all time
  • User Breakdown: Total users, senders, drivers, KYC pending/approved/rejected
  • Regional Distribution: Israeli cities mapped to regions (North, Center, South, Jerusalem, etc.)
  • Delivery Timings: Average time per status, pickup-to-delivery duration
  • Monthly Charts: Delivery volume, user growth, revenue trends
  • Cashflow: Total revenue, average delivery price, payment confirmation rate
  • CSV Export: Every data view supports CSV export with UTF-8 BOM for Hebrew support in Excel
Section 15 — Environments

15. Environments

AttributeDevelopmentProduction
Firebase Project IDmooviz-app-9b766mooviz-prod
Firebase Consoleconsole.firebase.google.com/project/mooviz-app-9b766console.firebase.google.com/project/mooviz-prod
Admin Dashboardmooviz-app-9b766.web.appadmin.mooviz.co.il
Cloud FunctionsDeployedDeployed
Firestore RulesDeployedDeployed
Storage RulesDeployedDeployed
Firebase Auth Ownertamir@k-a-l.solutionstamir@k-a-l.solutions
SMTP Serversmtp.hostinger.com:465 (SSL) — admin@mooviz.co.il
Google Maps API KeyStored in .env (gitignored) — loaded via react-native-config
Environment Files The following files are gitignored and must be configured manually on new development machines:
  • apps/mobile/.env — GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY
  • apps/mobile/android/app/google-services.json — Firebase Android config
  • apps/mobile/ios/MoovizMobile/GoogleService-Info.plist — Firebase iOS config
  • apps/admin/.env.production — Firebase production project config

Firebase Project Configuration

The firebase.json at the project root configures:

  • Firestore: Rules file (firestore.rules), indexes
  • Storage: Rules file (storage.rules)
  • Functions: Source directory (functions), Node 18 runtime
  • Hosting: Admin dashboard public directory, SPA rewrites
Section 16 — Build & Deploy

16. Build & Deploy

16.1   Android Build

# Navigate to mobile app
cd apps/mobile/android

# Clean build
./gradlew clean

# Build release APK (signed)
./gradlew assembleRelease

# Output location:
# apps/mobile/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/mooviz-{version}.apk
ConfigValue
compileSdk34
targetSdk34
minSdk24 (Android 7.0)
Android Gradle Plugin8.5
JS EngineHermes
Page Alignment16KB (required for modern Android)
SigningRelease keystore configured in build.gradle

16.2   iOS Build

# Navigate to iOS project
cd apps/mobile/ios

# Install CocoaPods dependencies
pod install

# Open in Xcode
open MoovizMobile.xcworkspace

# In Xcode:
# 1. Select "MoovizMobile" scheme
# 2. Select "Any iOS Device (arm64)" target
# 3. Product → Archive
# 4. Distribute to TestFlight
ConfigValue
Firebase iOS SDK11.11
CocoaPodsModular headers (selective)
Deployment TargetiOS 15.0+
APNsRequired for push notifications and phone auth
SigningApple Developer Program required

16.3   Cloud Functions Deploy

# Build shared package first (if validators changed)
cd shared
pnpm build
pnpm pack
# Copy mooviz-shared-1.0.0.tgz to functions/
cp mooviz-shared-1.0.0.tgz ../functions/
cd ../functions
pnpm install

# Deploy to development
firebase deploy --only functions --project mooviz-app-9b766

# Deploy to production
firebase deploy --only functions --project mooviz-prod
Shared Package Rebuild When updating validators or types in shared/, you MUST rebuild the tgz package. The predeploy cp command does NOT update node_modules/@mooviz/shared which comes from the tgz. Sequence: cd shared && pnpm build && pnpm pack → copy tgz → cd functions && pnpm install → deploy.

16.4   Admin Dashboard Deploy

# Build for production
cd apps/admin
pnpm build --mode mooviz-prod

# Deploy to Firebase Hosting
firebase deploy --only hosting --project mooviz-prod

16.5   Firestore & Storage Rules Deploy

# Deploy rules to development
firebase deploy --only firestore:rules,storage --project mooviz-app-9b766

# Deploy rules to production
firebase deploy --only firestore:rules,storage --project mooviz-prod

16.6   Metro Bundler (Development)

# Start Metro bundler for React Native development
cd apps/mobile
node ../../node_modules/react-native/cli.js start

# Run on Android emulator/device
node ../../node_modules/react-native/cli.js run-android

# Run on iOS simulator
node ../../node_modules/react-native/cli.js run-ios

16.7   Git Workflow

AttributeValue
Source ControlGitHub (private repository)
Branchesmain, develop, feature/*, release/*
Commit Format<type>(<scope>): <description>
Commit Typesfeat, fix, refactor, style, docs, test, chore
Monorepopnpm workspaces
Remotesorigin (developer), client (client)
Section 17 — Known Limitations & Future Work

17. Known Limitations & Future Work

17.1   Current Limitations

AreaLimitationImpact
Payments No in-app payment processing. External deep links to Bit/PayBox apps only. Users must manually confirm payment receipt. No automated payment verification.
Shared Package shared/ imports firebase-admin (server SDK), causing friction when used from client SDK. Must use tgz bundle approach for functions. Cannot import shared types directly in mobile app without workarounds.
Glide Migration Migrated drivers have driverUnlocked: true without completing KYC process. These drivers bypass KYC checks via the grandfather clause in assertDriverApproved().
Firestore Rules Some delivery fields still allow direct client writes. adminActions collection has no security rules. Full RBAC lockdown is a P0 item for production hardening.
Edit/Delete Delivery No ability to edit or delete a delivery after creation. Users must cancel and recreate. Feature deferred to post-launch.
Store Submissions Apple App Store and Google Play Store submissions are pending. Distribution currently via direct APK install (Android) and TestFlight (iOS).
Monetization Zero monetization features. No commission, no subscription, no ads. Revenue model must be defined for sustainability.
Privacy Policy Privacy policy page referenced but not fully implemented. Required for store submissions (Apple and Google both require it).
iOS OTP Testing Phone OTP verification requires physical device + APNs certificate. Cannot test on iOS Simulator. Must use real device for phone auth testing.

17.2   Future Work (Backlog)

FeaturePriorityDescription
Production Config P0 Separate Firebase production project with dedicated signing keys.
Store Submissions P0 Apple App Store + Google Play Store submission with required assets, screenshots, privacy policy.
Full RBAC Lockdown P0 Complete Firestore security rules hardening. Remove direct client write access to protected fields.
Bug Triage P0 Pre-release P0/P1 bug triage and resolution.
Analytics Events P1 GA4/Amplitude event tracking across all user actions for product analytics.
Interstitial Ads P1 Ad integration for monetization (post-launch).
Edit/Delete Delivery P1 Allow senders to edit delivery details or delete before driver assignment.
Payment Integration P2 In-app payment processing to replace external deep links.
Delivery Tracking Map P2 Enhanced real-time tracking with ETA and route visualization.
Multi-Language P2 Full Arabic and Russian language support (current: Hebrew primary, English secondary).
Driver Vehicle Management P3 Vehicle type registration, capacity matching, vehicle photos.
Recurring Deliveries P3 Schedule recurring delivery requests (daily, weekly).

17.3   Technical Debt

  • Shared package architecture: The @mooviz/shared package should be split into @mooviz/types (platform-agnostic) and @mooviz/server-utils (firebase-admin dependent) to eliminate the server SDK friction on the client.
  • Firestore index management: Some compound queries require manually created Firestore indexes. These should be documented in firestore.indexes.json.
  • Error tracking: No Sentry/Crashlytics integration for production crash reporting. Firebase Crashlytics SDK is installed but not fully configured.
  • Test coverage: No automated test suite (unit, integration, or E2E). Testing has been manual QA rounds.
  • Rate limiting: Cloud Functions have basic rate limiting but no WAF or advanced DDoS protection.
Appendix

Appendix A — Configuration Constants

Key application constants defined in apps/mobile/src/constants/config.ts:

ConstantValueDescription
MAX_DELIVERY_RADIUS_KM50Maximum delivery search radius (km)
DEFAULT_DELIVERY_RADIUS_KM10Default search radius (km)
GEOHASH_PRECISION6Geohash precision for location indexing (~1.2km grid)
IMAGE_MAX_SIZE5,242,880 (5MB)Maximum image file size in bytes
IMAGE_MAX_WIDTH / HEIGHT1920Maximum image dimensions for upload
IMAGE_QUALITY0.8JPEG compression quality (0-1)
OTP_LENGTH6OTP code digit count
OTP_RESEND_COOLDOWN_SECONDS60Seconds between OTP resend attempts
CHAT_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH1,000Maximum characters per chat message
CHAT_MESSAGES_PAGE_SIZE50Messages loaded per pagination page
DELIVERIES_PAGE_SIZE20Deliveries loaded per pagination page
RATING_SCALE5Maximum star rating value
LOCATION_IDLE_INTERVAL_MS300,000 (5min)GPS tracking interval in idle mode
LOCATION_ACTIVE_INTERVAL_MS60,000 (1min)GPS tracking interval during active delivery
LOCATION_DISTANCE_FILTER_IDLE200mMinimum distance change for idle updates
LOCATION_DISTANCE_FILTER_ACTIVE50mMinimum distance change for active updates
APP_VERSION1.1.9Current application version
DEEP_LINK_SCHEMEmooviz://App deep link URL scheme
SUPPORT_EMAILsupport@mooviz.appSupport contact email
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_HOURS24Default delivery timeout (hours)

Appendix B — DeliveryStatus Type Reference

type DeliveryStatus =
  | "new"
  | "pending"
  | "awaiting_confirm"
  | "waiting_for_pickup"
  | "picked_up"
  | "delivered"
  | "awaiting_payment"
  | "completed_paid"
  | "cancelled";

type InterestedDriverStatus =
  | "interested"
  | "selected"
  | "confirmed"
  | "declined"
  | "cancelled"
  | "withdrawn";

interface InterestedDriver {
  uid: string;
  name: string;
  photoUrl: string | null;
  rating: number;
  completedDeliveries: number;
  distanceKm: number;
  expressedAt: Timestamp;
  status: InterestedDriverStatus;
}

interface StatusEntry {
  status: DeliveryStatus;
  timestamp: Timestamp;
  actor: string;
  note?: string;
}

// Terminal statuses (no further transitions allowed)
const TERMINAL_STATUSES: DeliveryStatus[] = ["cancelled", "completed_paid"];

// Statuses eligible for timeout cleanup
const TIMEOUT_ELIGIBLE_STATUSES: DeliveryStatus[] = [
  "new", "pending", "awaiting_confirm", "awaiting_payment"
];

Appendix C — User Type Reference

type UserRole = "sender" | "driver";
type ActiveMode = "client" | "driver";
type KycStatus = "pending" | "approved" | "rejected";
type UserStatus = "active" | "suspended" | "blocked";

interface User {
  uid: string;
  fullName: string;
  nickname?: string;
  phone: string;             // E.164 format
  email?: string;
  city: string;
  role: UserRole;
  activeMode: ActiveMode;
  driverAvailable: boolean;
  driverUnlocked: boolean;
  profilePhotoURL: string;
  kycDocumentURL: string;
  kycStatus: KycStatus;
  ratingAsDriver: { average: number; count: number };
  ratingAsSender: { average: number; count: number };
  completedDeliveries: number;
  status: UserStatus;
  fcmTokens: string[];
  location: { lat: number; lng: number; geohash: string };
  migratedFrom?: string;
  createdAt: Timestamp;
  updatedAt?: Timestamp;
}

Appendix D — Key File Paths Quick Reference

PurposePath
Firebase configfirebase.json
Firestore security rulesfirestore.rules
Storage security rulesstorage.rules
Mobile entry pointapps/mobile/index.js
Root Navigatorapps/mobile/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx
Firebase initialization (mobile)apps/mobile/src/services/firebase.ts
Auth serviceapps/mobile/src/services/auth.ts
App constantsapps/mobile/src/constants/config.ts
Status display configapps/mobile/src/constants/statusConfig.ts
Theme tokensapps/mobile/src/theme/tokens.ts
i18n stringsapps/mobile/src/i18n/strings.ts
Delivery callable functionsfunctions/src/callable/deliveryCallable.ts
User callable functionsfunctions/src/callable/userCallable.ts
Status validatorfunctions/src/validators/statusValidator.ts
Notification servicefunctions/src/services/notificationService.ts
Delivery triggerfunctions/src/triggers/deliveryTrigger.ts
Status transitions mapshared/src/constants/statuses.ts
Delivery typesshared/src/types/delivery.ts
User typesshared/src/types/user.ts
Android build configapps/mobile/android/app/build.gradle
iOS Podfileapps/mobile/ios/Podfile
Admin dashboard entryapps/admin/src/main.tsx
Shared package exportsshared/src/index.ts
Sound service (crash-proof)apps/mobile/src/services/sound.ts
Location tracking hookapps/mobile/src/hooks/useDriverLocationTracking.ts
Chat auto-close cronfunctions/src/scheduled/chatAutoClose.ts
Session continuation guidedocs/next-session.md

MOOVIZ — Technical Handoff Documentation
